== Overview

The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.

BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.

For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.

== Tabtarget Vesture

[[bust_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{bust_tabtarget}::
Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
+
* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
* separator: `.`
* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
* envelope: `.sh`
+
Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-cr.Rack.sh` — RBW workbench, Rack operation
* `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation

== Dispatch

The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
A
{busdl_launcher}
validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
The
{busdc_colophon}
identifies the routing path;
the
{busdf_frontispiece}
provides human-readable description;
the
{busdi_imprint}
embeds target parameters.

[[busdc_colophon]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdc_colophon}::
The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdc_colophon}
determines which
{busdm_formulary}
handles the dispatch.
Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
+
The
{busdc_colophon}
is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench

[[busdf_frontispiece]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdf_frontispiece}::
The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdf_frontispiece}
uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
+
Appears after the
{busdc_colophon}
and dot separator.
+
Examples: `Build`, `Rack`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.

[[busdi_imprint]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdi_imprint}::
An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
An
{busdi_imprint}
specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
+
Appears after a second dot separator following the
{busdf_frontispiece}.
+
Examples:
+
* `tadmor` in `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
* `srjcl` in `rbw-cr.Rack.srjcl.sh`

[[busdm_formulary]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busdm_formulary}::
A component that routes
{busdc_colophon_s}
to implementations.
Each
{busdm_formulary}
registers handlers for a set of
{busdc_colophon_s}
and dispatches to the appropriate function.
+
Types:
+
* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
+
A
{busdm_formulary}
is sourced by the
{busdl_launcher}
and provides the dispatch function.

[[busdl_launcher]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busdl_launcher}::
A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
A
{busdl_launcher}
is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
+
Naming follows
{bust_tabtarget}:
`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Responsibilities:
+
* Parse
{busdc_colophon}
from own filename
* Validate required arguments
* Source the target
{busdm_formulary}
* Invoke the dispatched function

content.content:
400	
401	== Routing Behavior
402	
403	=== Dispatch Flow
404	
405	. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
406	. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
407	* {busdc_colophon}:
408	`rbw-cr`
409	* {busdf_frontispiece}:
410	`Rack`
411	* {busdi_imprint}:
412	`tadmor`
413	. Launcher sources the appropriate
414	{busdm_formulary}
415	(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
416	. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
417	. Target function executes with imprint as parameter
418	
419	=== Workbench Pattern
420	
421	[source,bash]
422	----
423	# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh
424	
425	{prefix}w_dispatch() {
426	    local frontispiece="$1"
427	    local imprint="$2"
428	    shift 2
429	
430	    case "$frontispiece" in
431	        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
432	        Rack)           {prefix}w_rack "$imprint" "$@" ;;
433	        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
434	    esac
435	}
436	----
437	
438	=== Testbench Pattern
439	
440	Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:
441	
442	[source,bash]
443	----
444	# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh
445	
446	{prefix}t_dispatch() {
447	    local frontispiece="$1"
448	    shift
449	
450	    case "$frontispiece" in
451	        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
452	        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
453	        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
454	    esac
455	}
456	----
457	
458	=== Test Registry Model
459	
460	The test registry (`butr_registry.sh`) uses two orthogonal enrollment dimensions:
461	
462	**Fixture** — init/setup execution context.
463	Each test case belongs to exactly one fixture.
464	Fixtures provide isolation via `_tsuite` subshell boundaries.
465	Registered with `butr_fixture_enroll(name, init_fn, setup_fn)`.
466	
467	**Suite** — cross-cutting selection group (N:M with cases).
468	A case can belong to multiple suites.
469	Suites classify by dependency tier (what environment must exist), not by test methodology.
470	A suite is a membership tag.
471	Suites enable suite dispatch: run a cross-cutting subset of cases grouped by their owning fixtures.
472	Suite context is set with `butr_suite_enroll(SUITE1 [SUITE2...])` before case enrollments.
473	Queried by `butd_run_suite(suite_name)`.
474	
475	Standard suite constants (defined as `readonly` in testbench kindle):
476	
477	* `BUTR_SUITE_FAST` — no external dependencies, CI-safe
478	* `BUTR_SUITE_SERVICE` — needs credentials, no container runtime
479	* `BUTR_SUITE_COMPLETE` — full test matrix including container runtime
480	
481	Dispatch entry points:
482	
483	* `butd_run_all()` — all fixtures, all cases
484	* `butd_run_fixture(name [case...])` — single fixture, optional case filter
485	* `butd_run_one(fn)` — single case with its fixture setup
486	* `butd_run_suite(suite)` — cross-cutting suite, grouped by fixture
487	
488	=== Colophon Conventions
489	
490	{busdc_colophon_s}
491	follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:
492	
493	* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
494	* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
495	* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-cr-` for crucible rack operations)
496	
497	The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.
498	
499	== Test Invocation
500	
501	The test invocation vocabulary defines how test cases exercise code under controlled capture.
502	All invocation tiers share a common primitive,
503	`zbuto_invoke`,
504	which captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and optional BURV output into
505	{busig_stdout},
506	{busig_stderr},
507	{busig_status},
508	and
509	{busig_burv_output}.
510	
511	=== Invocation Tiers
512	
513	Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:
514	
515	[[busit_unit]]
516	//axl_voices axo_entity
517	{busit_unit}::
518	Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
519	No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
520	+
521	API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`
522	
523	[[busit_tabtarget]]
524	//axl_voices axo_entity
525	{busit_tabtarget}::
526	Resolves a
527	{busdc_colophon}
528	to a
529	{busdl_launcher}
530	file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
531	Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
532	+
533	API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`
534	
535	[[busit_launch]]
536	//axl_voices axo_entity
537	{busit_launch}::
538	Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
539	{busdl_launcher}
540	file.
541	Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
542	+
543	API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`
544	
545	=== Capture Globals
546	
547	Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.
548	
549	[[busig_stdout]]
550	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
551	{busig_stdout}::
552	Captured stdout from the invoked command.
553	
554	[[busig_stderr]]
555	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
556	{busig_stderr}::
557	Captured stderr from the invoked command.
558	
559	[[busig_status]]
560	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
561	{busig_status}::
562	Exit code from the invoked command.
563	
564	[[busig_burv_output]]
565	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
566	{busig_burv_output}::
567	BURV output directory for the invocation.
568	Empty when
569	{busx_burv_root}
570	is not set.
571	When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
572	`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.
573	
574	=== Case Boundary
575	
576	[[busx_tcase]]
577	//axl_voices axo_entity
578	{busx_tcase}::
579	The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
580	Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
581	Sets
582	{busx_temp_dir}
583	and
584	{busx_burv_root}
585	before invoking the case function.
586	+
587	The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.
588	
589	[[busx_temp_dir]]
590	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
591	{busx_temp_dir}::
592	Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
593	{busx_tcase}.
594	Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.
595	
596	[[busx_burv_root]]
597	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
598	{busx_burv_root}::
599	Per-case BURV isolation root (`BUTE_BURV_ROOT`), set by
600	{busx_tcase}.
601	When present, all invocation tiers create per-invoke BURV directories under this root, enabling multi-step test sequences to produce and consume isolated output.
602	
603	=== Fact-File Pattern
604	
605	[[busff_fact_file]]
606	//axl_voices axo_entity
607	{busff_fact_file}::
608	Inter-step communication pattern using named output files.
609	+
610	A producer writes a value to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${CONSTANT}` during command execution.
611	A consumer reads it from `${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${CONSTANT}` after invocation.
612	The kindle constant name is the contract between producer and consumer.
613	+
614	Example:
615	+
616	[source,bash]
617	----
618	# Producer (in command implementation):
619	echo "${z_hallmark}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}"
620	
621	# Consumer (in test case, after buto_tt_expect_ok):
622	z_hallmark=$(<"${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}")
623	----
624	+
625	The `current/` path segment maps
626	{burd_output_dir}
627	inside the BURV isolation tree.
628	Fact-file constants are defined as `readonly` in kindle (e.g., `RBF_FACT_HALLMARK`).
629	
630	[[busff_multi_fact]]
631	//axl_voices axo_entity
632	{busff_multi_fact}::
633	Inter-step communication pattern for emitting multiple facts of a uniform shape during a single dispatch.
634	Each fact lands as a file named `<root>.<extension>` under
635	{burd_output_dir}
636	(and durably under
637	{burd_temp_dir}),
638	where the extension classifies the fact type and the root identifies the per-row instance.
639	+
640	The pattern composes three rules:
641	+
642	* *Type-as-extension naming.* The extension token carries the fact's classification; the root carries the row identity.
643	A producer emitting per-alias observation facts writes `<alias>.buf_ext_alias` rather than encoding the type into the filename stem.
644	* *Closed registry.* Each allowed extension is a named constant of the shape `BUF_EXT_<NAME>` declared in `buf_fact.sh`.
645	Constant values match constant names downcased (e.g., `BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`), so any fact file is recognizable on disk by extension alone.
646	Adding an extension is a deliberate edit to the registry; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape.
647	* *BUK/RB boundary discipline.* The BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions.
648	RB-domain extensions register in `rbgc_Constants.sh` under the `RBGP_FACT_EXT_*` family, and neither universe enumerates the other.
649	Boundary is by ownership of the producer module, not by where the fact lands on disk.
650	+
651	Emission cardinality: one write per `<root, extension>` key per dispatch.
652	A second write of the same key during one dispatch is a bug — the implementing primitive
653	{buf_write_fact_multi}
654	fails fatally on preexistence to surface it.
655	+
656	Semantically distinct from
657	{busff_fact_file}:
658	single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (the filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (the extension is the contract, the root is per-row).
659	
660	[[buf_write_fact_single]]
661	//axl_voices axo_entity
662	{buf_write_fact_single}::
663	Dual-write fact-file primitive — single-form.
664	Writes a named file to both
665	{burd_output_dir}
666	and
667	{burd_temp_dir}.
668	Dies if either copy already exists — a preexisting file indicates a double-write bug.
669	+
670	{burd_output_dir}
671	is the latest-command convenience copy, cleared on next dispatch.
672	{burd_temp_dir}
673	is the durable copy, addressable by anyone who captured the path.
674	+
675	All single-form fact-file producers must use
676	{buf_write_fact_single}
677	to ensure durable copies exist; multi-row producers use
678	{buf_write_fact_multi}.
679	+
680	[source,bash]
681	----
682	# Single-value fact:
683	buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"
684	
685	# Single-slot fact whose body has structured content (e.g., burx.env):
686	buf_write_fact_single "${BUF_burx_env}" "${z_content}"
687	----
688	
689	[[buf_write_fact_multi]]
690	//axl_voices axo_entity
691	{buf_write_fact_multi}::
692	Dual-write fact-file primitive — multi-form, implementing the
693	{busff_multi_fact}
694	pattern.
695	Writes a value to `<file_root>.<ext>` in both
696	{burd_output_dir}
697	and
698	{burd_temp_dir}.
699	Dies if either copy already exists.
700	+
701	Signature: `buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>`.
702	The extension is an opaque pass-through — the function performs no registry validation; the registered `BUF_EXT_*` constant at the call site is the contract, and out-of-registry usage is a producer-side bug to be caught by code review, not by this primitive.
703	+
704	[source,bash]
705	----
706	# Per-row emission inside a producer loop:
707	for z_alias in "${z_aliases[@]}"; do
708	  buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
709	done
710	----
711	+
712	Mirror of
713	{buf_write_fact_single}
714	for the multi-row shape; both use the same dual-write + fail-on-preexist semantics.
715	
716	[[buf_burx_env]]
717	//axpof_fact
718	{buf_burx_env}::
719	Tinder constant naming the
720	{burx_regime}
721	fact-file (`burx.env`).
722	Written by bud_dispatch.sh via
723	{buf_write_fact_single}
724	at dispatch start, with terminal fields appended at completion.
725	
726	[[buf_ext_alias]]
727	//axl_voices axt_enum_value
728	{buf_ext_alias}::
729	Registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURN alias observation facts (`BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`).
730	Written by `burn_list_capture` (in `burn_regime.sh`) once per discovered alias profile, with empty content — presence-of-file is the fact, not file content.
731	+
732	First and currently sole member of the
733	{busff_multi_fact}
734	registry; serves as the proof-of-shape for the convention.
735	
736	=== Yelp Pattern
737	
738	[[busy_yelp]]
739	//axl_voices axk_premise
740	{busy_yelp}::
741	A formatted string fragment for interpolation into `buh_line` calls.
742	Yelp content **must not contain literal `%` characters** — this premise allows safe interpolation into `printf` format strings via bash variable expansion.
743	+
744	Produced by
745	{busy_yawp_s}
746	via pure assignment of inert
747	{busy_diastema}
748	markers, resolved at display time by
749	{busy_format_yawp}.
750	+
751	Yelps are the vocabulary-first primitive for handbook output.
752	A kit defines vocabulary constants at kindle time by capturing yelps into readonly globals (e.g., `RBYC_DEPOT`, `RBYC_HALLMARK`).
753	Call sites compose lines by interpolating these constants into `buh_line` strings.
754	
755	[[busy_yawp]]
756	//axl_voices axo_entity
757	{busy_yawp}::
758	A `buyy_*_yawp` function in `buym_yelp.sh` that produces a
759	{busy_yelp}
760	via pure bash assignment into the group return variable `z_buym_yelp`.
761	No subshells, no stdout, no process spawning — cannot fail.
762	+
763	Yawp functions stamp inert
764	{busy_diastema}
765	markers into strings.
766	All terminal capability decisions are deferred to
767	{busy_format_yawp}
768	at display time.
769	Eight yawp functions exist: `buyy_cmd_yawp` (command), `buyy_ui_yawp` (UI element), `buyy_href_yawp` (web hyperlink), `buyy_link_yawp` (vocabulary link), `buyy_tt_yawp` (resolved tabtarget), `buyy_pass_yawp` (pass/green), `buyy_warn_yawp` (warn/orange), `buyy_fail_yawp` (fail/red).
770	
771	[[busy_diastema]]
772	//axl_voices axo_entity
773	{busy_diastema}::
774	A non-printing byte sequence (`\x02\x1N`) embedded in a
775	{busy_yelp}
776	by a
777	{busy_yawp}
778	to delimit styled regions.
779	Eight markers form the diastema family: CMD, UI, HREF_URL, HREF_TEXT, LINK_URL, LINK_TEXT, TT, and END.
780	Openers identify the region type; END returns to the ambient color.
781	HREF and LINK have separate markers because vocabulary links (teaching terms) and web links (external references) serve different pedagogical purposes and carry independent palette slots.
782	
783	[[busy_format_yawp]]
784	//axl_voices axo_entity
785	{busy_format_yawp}::
786	The single intelligence point for yelp display: `buyf_format_yawp(color, string)`.
787	Takes a `BUYC_*` ambient color constant and a
788	{busy_diastema}-marked
789	string, resolves all markers into ANSI color sequences and OSC-8 hyperlinks, and sets `z_buym_format`.
790	+
791	Resolution order: structured markers (HREF, LINK) via BASH_REMATCH extraction, then simple markers (CMD, UI, TT) via string replacement, then END markers replaced with the ambient color.
792	
793	[[busy_configurator]]
794	//axl_voices axo_entity
795	{busy_configurator}::
796	A `buyc_*` function called before kindle to set the formatting mode flag.
797	Three configurators exist: `buyc_dispatch` (probe TERM/NO_COLOR/BURD_*), `buyc_unconditional` (always color), `buyc_plain` (never color).
798	Kindle reads the mode flag and defines the readonly `BUYC_*` public color palette and hyperlink mode.
799

file.content:
== Routing Behavior

=== Dispatch Flow

. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
* {busdc_colophon}:
`rbw-cr`
* {busdf_frontispiece}:
`Rack`
* {busdi_imprint}:
`tadmor`
. Launcher sources the appropriate
{busdm_formulary}
(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
. Target function executes with imprint as parameter

=== Workbench Pattern

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh

{prefix}w_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    local imprint="$2"
    shift 2

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        Rack)           {prefix}w_rack "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Testbench Pattern

Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh

{prefix}t_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    shift

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Test Registry Model

The test registry (`butr_registry.sh`) uses two orthogonal enrollment dimensions:

**Fixture** — init/setup execution context.
Each test case belongs to exactly one fixture.
Fixtures provide isolation via `_tsuite` subshell boundaries.
Registered with `butr_fixture_enroll(name, init_fn, setup_fn)`.

**Suite** — cross-cutting selection group (N:M with cases).
A case can belong to multiple suites.
Suites classify by dependency tier (what environment must exist), not by test methodology.
A suite is a membership tag.
Suites enable suite dispatch: run a cross-cutting subset of cases grouped by their owning fixtures.
Suite context is set with `butr_suite_enroll(SUITE1 [SUITE2...])` before case enrollments.
Queried by `butd_run_suite(suite_name)`.

Standard suite constants (defined as `readonly` in testbench kindle):

* `BUTR_SUITE_FAST` — no external dependencies, CI-safe
* `BUTR_SUITE_SERVICE` — needs credentials, no container runtime
* `BUTR_SUITE_COMPLETE` — full test matrix including container runtime

Dispatch entry points:

* `butd_run_all()` — all fixtures, all cases
* `butd_run_fixture(name [case...])` — single fixture, optional case filter
* `butd_run_one(fn)` — single case with its fixture setup
* `butd_run_suite(suite)` — cross-cutting suite, grouped by fixture

=== Colophon Conventions

{busdc_colophon_s}
follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:

* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-cr-` for crucible rack operations)

The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.

== Test Invocation

The test invocation vocabulary defines how test cases exercise code under controlled capture.
All invocation tiers share a common primitive,
`zbuto_invoke`,
which captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and optional BURV output into
{busig_stdout},
{busig_stderr},
{busig_status},
and
{busig_burv_output}.

=== Invocation Tiers

Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:

[[busit_unit]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_unit}::
Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
+
API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`

[[busit_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_tabtarget}::
Resolves a
{busdc_colophon}
to a
{busdl_launcher}
file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
+
API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`

[[busit_launch]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_launch}::
Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
{busdl_launcher}
file.
Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
+
API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`

=== Capture Globals

Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.

[[busig_stdout]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stdout}::
Captured stdout from the invoked command.

[[busig_stderr]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stderr}::
Captured stderr from the invoked command.

[[busig_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{busig_status}::
Exit code from the invoked command.

[[busig_burv_output]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busig_burv_output}::
BURV output directory for the invocation.
Empty when
{busx_burv_root}
is not set.
When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.

=== Case Boundary

[[busx_tcase]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busx_tcase}::
The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
Sets
{busx_temp_dir}
and
{busx_burv_root}
before invoking the case function.
+
The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.

[[busx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_temp_dir}::
Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.

[[busx_burv_root]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_burv_root}::
Per-case BURV isolation root (`BUTE_BURV_ROOT`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
When present, all invocation tiers create per-invoke BURV directories under this root, enabling multi-step test sequences to produce and consume isolated output.

=== Fact-File Pattern

[[busff_fact_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busff_fact_file}::
Inter-step communication pattern using named output files.
+
A producer writes a value to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${CONSTANT}` during command execution.
A consumer reads it from `${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${CONSTANT}` after invocation.
The kindle constant name is the contract between producer and consumer.
+
Example:
+
[source,bash]
----
# Producer (in command implementation):
echo "${z_hallmark}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}"

# Consumer (in test case, after buto_tt_expect_ok):
z_hallmark=$(<"${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}")
----
+
The `current/` path segment maps
{burd_output_dir}
inside the BURV isolation tree.
Fact-file constants are defined as `readonly` in kindle (e.g., `RBF_FACT_HALLMARK`).

[[busff_multi_fact]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busff_multi_fact}::
Inter-step communication pattern for emitting multiple facts of a uniform shape during a single dispatch.
Each fact lands as a file named `<root>.<extension>` under
{burd_output_dir}
(and durably under
{burd_temp_dir}),
where the extension classifies the fact type and the root identifies the per-row instance.
+
The pattern composes three rules:
+
* *Type-as-extension naming.* The extension token carries the fact's classification; the root carries the row identity.
A producer emitting per-alias observation facts writes `<alias>.buf_ext_alias` rather than encoding the type into the filename stem.
* *Closed registry.* Each allowed extension is a named constant of the shape `BUF_EXT_<NAME>` declared in `buf_fact.sh`.
Constant values match constant names downcased (e.g., `BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`), so any fact file is recognizable on disk by extension alone.
Adding an extension is a deliberate edit to the registry; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape.
* *BUK/RB boundary discipline.* The BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions.
RB-domain extensions register in `rbgc_Constants.sh` under the `RBGP_FACT_EXT_*` family, and neither universe enumerates the other.
Boundary is by ownership of the producer module, not by where the fact lands on disk.
+
Emission cardinality: one write per `<root, extension>` key per dispatch.
A second write of the same key during one dispatch is a bug — the implementing primitive
{buf_write_fact_multi}
fails fatally on preexistence to surface it.
+
Semantically distinct from
{busff_fact_file}:
single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (the filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (the extension is the contract, the root is per-row).

[[buf_write_fact_single]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{buf_write_fact_single}::
Dual-write fact-file primitive — single-form.
Writes a named file to both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}.
Dies if either copy already exists — a preexisting file indicates a double-write bug.
+
{burd_output_dir}
is the latest-command convenience copy, cleared on next dispatch.
{burd_temp_dir}
is the durable copy, addressable by anyone who captured the path.
+
All single-form fact-file producers must use
{buf_write_fact_single}
to ensure durable copies exist; multi-row producers use
{buf_write_fact_multi}.
+
[source,bash]
----
# Single-value fact:
buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"

# Single-slot fact whose body has structured content (e.g., burx.env):
buf_write_fact_single "${BUF_burx_env}" "${z_content}"
----

[[buf_write_fact_multi]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{buf_write_fact_multi}::
Dual-write fact-file primitive — multi-form, implementing the
{busff_multi_fact}
pattern.
Writes a value to `<file_root>.<ext>` in both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}.
Dies if either copy already exists.
+
Signature: `buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>`.
The extension is an opaque pass-through — the function performs no registry validation; the registered `BUF_EXT_*` constant at the call site is the contract, and out-of-registry usage is a producer-side bug to be caught by code review, not by this primitive.
+
[source,bash]
----
# Per-row emission inside a producer loop:
for z_alias in "${z_aliases[@]}"; do
  buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
done
----
+
Mirror of
{buf_write_fact_single}
for the multi-row shape; both use the same dual-write + fail-on-preexist semantics.

[[buf_burx_env]]
//axpof_fact
{buf_burx_env}::
Tinder constant naming the
{burx_regime}
fact-file (`burx.env`).
Written by bud_dispatch.sh via
{buf_write_fact_single}
at dispatch start, with terminal fields appended at completion.

[[buf_ext_alias]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{buf_ext_alias}::
Registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURN alias observation facts (`BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`).
Written by `burn_list_capture` (in `burn_regime.sh`) once per discovered alias profile, with empty content — presence-of-file is the fact, not file content.
+
First and currently sole member of the
{busff_multi_fact}
registry; serves as the proof-of-shape for the convention.

=== Yelp Pattern

[[busy_yelp]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{busy_yelp}::
A formatted string fragment for interpolation into `buh_line` calls.
Yelp content **must not contain literal `%` characters** — this premise allows safe interpolation into `printf` format strings via bash variable expansion.
+
Produced by
{busy_yawp_s}
via pure assignment of inert
{busy_diastema}
markers, resolved at display time by
{busy_format_yawp}.
+
Yelps are the vocabulary-first primitive for handbook output.
A kit defines vocabulary constants at kindle time by capturing yelps into readonly globals (e.g., `RBYC_DEPOT`, `RBYC_HALLMARK`).
Call sites compose lines by interpolating these constants into `buh_line` strings.

[[busy_yawp]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_yawp}::
A `buyy_*_yawp` function in `buym_yelp.sh` that produces a
{busy_yelp}
via pure bash assignment into the group return variable `z_buym_yelp`.
No subshells, no stdout, no process spawning — cannot fail.
+
Yawp functions stamp inert
{busy_diastema}
markers into strings.
All terminal capability decisions are deferred to
{busy_format_yawp}
at display time.
Eight yawp functions exist: `buyy_cmd_yawp` (command), `buyy_ui_yawp` (UI element), `buyy_href_yawp` (web hyperlink), `buyy_link_yawp` (vocabulary link), `buyy_tt_yawp` (resolved tabtarget), `buyy_pass_yawp` (pass/green), `buyy_warn_yawp` (warn/orange), `buyy_fail_yawp` (fail/red).

[[busy_diastema]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_diastema}::
A non-printing byte sequence (`\x02\x1N`) embedded in a
{busy_yelp}
by a
{busy_yawp}
to delimit styled regions.
Eight markers form the diastema family: CMD, UI, HREF_URL, HREF_TEXT, LINK_URL, LINK_TEXT, TT, and END.
Openers identify the region type; END returns to the ambient color.
HREF and LINK have separate markers because vocabulary links (teaching terms) and web links (external references) serve different pedagogical purposes and carry independent palette slots.

[[busy_format_yawp]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_format_yawp}::
The single intelligence point for yelp display: `buyf_format_yawp(color, string)`.
Takes a `BUYC_*` ambient color constant and a
{busy_diastema}-marked
string, resolves all markers into ANSI color sequences and OSC-8 hyperlinks, and sets `z_buym_format`.
+
Resolution order: structured markers (HREF, LINK) via BASH_REMATCH extraction, then simple markers (CMD, UI, TT) via string replacement, then END markers replaced with the ambient color.

[[busy_configurator]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_configurator}::
A `buyc_*` function called before kindle to set the formatting mode flag.
Three configurators exist: `buyc_dispatch` (probe TERM/NO_COLOR/BURD_*), `buyc_unconditional` (always color), `buyc_plain` (never color).
Kindle reads the mode flag and defines the readonly `BUYC_*` public color palette and hyperlink mode.

content.content:
800	== Regime Configuration
801	
802	BUK defines two
803	{axrg_regime_s}
804	for project and developer configuration.
805	{axrg_assignment_s}
806	use
807	{axf_bash}
808	format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).
809	
810	=== Regime Prefixes
811	
812	[[burc_prefix]]
813	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
814	{burc_prefix}::
815	Bash Utility Configuration Regime
816	
817	[[burs_prefix]]
818	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
819	{burs_prefix}::
820	Bash Utility Station Regime
821	
822	[[burn_prefix]]
823	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
824	{burn_prefix}::
825	Bash Utility Regime Node
826	
827	[[bure_prefix]]
828	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
829	{bure_prefix}::
830	Bash Utility Environment Regime
831	
832	=== Kit Regime Operations
833	
834	[[bukro_kindle]]
835	//axvr_kindle
836	{bukro_kindle}::
837	Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
838	{axrg_assignment_s}.
839	Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.
840	
841	[[bukro_validate]]
842	//axvr_validate
843	{bukro_validate}::
844	Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
845	{axrg_regime_s}.
846	Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.
847	
848	[[bukro_render]]
849	//axvr_render
850	{bukro_render}::
851	Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
852	{axrg_regime}
853	state.
854	
855	=== BURC Regime
856	
857	[[burc_regime]]
858	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
859	{burc_regime}::
860	Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
861	BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.
862	
863	[[burc_station_file]]
864	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
865	{burc_station_file}::
866	Path to the developer's personal
867	{burs_regime}
868	assignment file.
869	
870	[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
871	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
872	{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
873	Directory containing
874	{busdl_launcher}
875	scripts.
876	
877	[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
878	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
879	{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
880	Single-character token separator in
881	{bust_tabtarget}
882	inscriptions.
883	
884	[[burc_tools_dir]]
885	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
886	{burc_tools_dir}::
887	Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.
888	
889	[[burc_project_root]]
890	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
891	{burc_project_root}::
892	Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.
893	
894	[[burc_managed_kits]]
895	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
896	{burc_managed_kits}::
897	Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
898	At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
899	At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.
900	
901	[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
902	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
903	{burc_temp_root_dir}::
904	Root directory for BUK temporary files.
905	
906	[[burc_output_root_dir]]
907	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
908	{burc_output_root_dir}::
909	Root directory for BUK command output files.
910	
911	[[burc_log_last]]
912	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
913	{burc_log_last}::
914	Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.
915	
916	[[burc_log_ext]]
917	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
918	{burc_log_ext}::
919	File extension for log files, without leading dot.
920	
921	=== BURS Regime
922	
923	[[burs_regime]]
924	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
925	{burs_regime}::
926	Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
927	BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.
928	
929	[[burs_user]]
930	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
931	{burs_user}::
932	Local developer username.
933	Routes to the correct `.buk/rbmu_users/` subdirectory for
934	{burp_regime}
935	profiles.
936	
937	[[burs_log_dir]]
938	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
939	{burs_log_dir}::
940	Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.
941	
942	=== BURN Regime
943	
944	[[burn_regime]]
945	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
946	{burn_regime}::
947	Per-node configuration regime describing the shape of a remote target.
948	A
949	{burn_regime}
950	instance is identified by its
951	{burn_viceroyalty}
952	(directory name) and is shared across station users (git-transported);
953	it carries no key material and changes rarely.
954	+
955	{burn_regime}
956	files live under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/burn.env`.
957	+
958	{burn_regime}
959	is the sole home of node address-of-record per
960	{burn_host_singularity};
961	{burp_regime}
962	and
963	{burw_regime}
964	instances reference it via
965	{burp_node}
966	and
967	{burw_node}.
968	
969	[[burn_host]]
970	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
971	{burn_host}::
972	IP address or hostname of the remote node.
973	
974	[[burn_platform]]
975	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
976	{burn_platform}::
977	OS family of the node.
978	Selects procedure dispatch for OS-specific ceremonies
979	(garrison shell selection, sshd configuration paths, quoting regime).
980	Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.
981	+
982	Admin-shell variant on Windows nodes (cygwin / wsl / powershell) is
983	captured in
984	{burp_command}
985	per investiture, not at the node level — a single Windows host can
986	support multiple admin-shell stacks under separate
987	{burp_investiture}
988	instances.
989	Workload-shell variant is captured in
990	{burw_lieutenancy}
991	naming.
992	
993	[[burn_workloads]]
994	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated
995	{burn_workloads}::
996	Authoritative template of valid
997	{burw_lieutenancy}
998	strings on this node.
999	{busn_conscript}
1000	may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
1001	the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.
1002	
1003	=== BURN/BURP/BURW Identifier Vocabulary
1004	
1005	The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
1006	BURN/BURP/BURW model.
1007	
1008	[[burn_viceroyalty]]
1009	//axl_voices axo_entity
1010	{burn_viceroyalty}::
1011	The minted identifier of a
1012	{burn_regime}
1013	instance.
1014	{burp_regime}
1015	and
1016	{burw_regime}
1017	instances reference a node by its
1018	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1019	+
1020	The
1021	{burn_viceroyalty}
1022	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1023	{burn_regime}
1024	assignment file;
1025	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1026	+
1027	Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.
1028	
1029	[[burp_investiture]]
1030	//axl_voices axo_entity
1031	{burp_investiture}::
1032	The minted identifier of a
1033	{burp_regime}
1034	instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
1035	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1036	+
1037	The
1038	{burp_investiture}
1039	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1040	{burp_regime}
1041	assignment file;
1042	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1043	+
1044	Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.
1045	
1046	[[burw_lieutenancy]]
1047	//axl_voices axo_entity
1048	{burw_lieutenancy}::
1049	The minted identifier of a
1050	{burw_regime}
1051	instance — a workload office operating on a
1052	{burn_viceroyalty}
1053	under the authority of a
1054	{burp_investiture}.
1055	A
1056	{burw_lieutenancy}
1057	names a workload entry (shell command, remote OS user, key file) that
1058	materializes when a workload is conscripted.
1059	+
1060	The
1061	{burw_lieutenancy}
1062	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1063	{burw_regime}
1064	state file;
1065	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1066	+
1067	The
1068	{burw_lieutenancy}
1069	string is by construction the remote OS username of its workload
1070	account on the node, drawn at conscript time from the
1071	{burn_viceroyalty}'s authoritative `burn_workloads` template.
1072	+
1073	Examples: `redbox-cyg`, `redbox-wsl`, `redbox-pwsh`.
1074	
1075	[[burn_host_singularity]]
1076	//axl_voices axk_premise
1077	{burn_host_singularity}::
1078	The host of a node lives only in
1079	{burn_regime}.
1080	{burp_regime}
1081	and
1082	{burw_regime}
1083	instances reach the host by reference
1084	(via
1085	{burp_node}
1086	and
1087	{burw_node}
1088	respectively) and never duplicate it.
1089	A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
1090	credentials and workload state never re-state it.
1091	
1092	[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
1093	//axl_voices axk_premise
1094	{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
1095	The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
1096	The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
1097	distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
1098	keys on the operator's station.
1099	Regime files carry only the public-key line;
1100	private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
1101	BUK procedures.
1102	
1103	=== BURP Regime
1104	
1105	[[burp_prefix]]
1106	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1107	{burp_prefix}::
1108	Bash Utility Regime Privileged
1109	
1110	[[burp_regime]]
1111	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
1112	{burp_regime}::
1113	Per-station-user privileged-credential regime.
1114	A
1115	{burp_regime}
1116	instance is identified by its
1117	{burp_investiture}
1118	(directory name).
1119	+
1120	{burp_regime}
1121	files live under
1122	`.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/burp.env`.
1123	+
1124	{burp_regime}
1125	references its node by
1126	{burp_node}
1127	per
1128	{burn_host_singularity}.
1129	
1130	[[burp_node]]
1131	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1132	{burp_node}::
1133	Reference to a
1134	{burn_viceroyalty}
1135	— the node this investiture is granted over.
1136	Resolves to the
1137	{burn_regime}
1138	file at the directory bearing this name.
1139	
1140	[[burp_user]]
1141	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1142	{burp_user}::
1143	Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as
1144	(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
1145	Linux/Mac).
1146	
1147	[[burp_ssh_pubkey]]
1148	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1149	{burp_ssh_pubkey}::
1150	Full SSH public key line of the operator-managed admin keypair.
1151	The matching private key lives at `${BURS_KEY_DIR}/<{burp_investiture}>` on
1152	the operator's station per
1153	{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
1154	
1155	[[burp_key_file]]
1156	//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
1157	{burp_key_file}::
1158	Operator-managed SSH private key filename.
1159	Resolves under the operator's key directory (typically `~/.ssh/`) per
1160	{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
1161	Empty defaults to the
1162	{burp_investiture}
1163	(one key per investiture).
1164	
1165	[[burp_command]]
1166	//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
1167	{burp_command}::
1168	Shell command for SSH `command=` routing in the node's
1169	`administrators_authorized_keys`.
1170	Empty for direct-shell connections;
1171	non-empty when the admin shell is selected at the SSH layer
1172	(e.g., `powershell.exe`, `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l`).
1173	
1174	=== BURW Regime
1175	
1176	[[burw_prefix]]
1177	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1178	{burw_prefix}::
1179	Bash Utility Regime Workload
1180	
1181	[[burw_regime]]
1182	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
1183	{burw_regime}::
1184	Per-station-user workload-state regime — a record of a workload office
1185	materialized on a node by
1186	{busn_conscript}.
1187	A
1188	{burw_regime}
1189	instance is identified by its
1190	{burw_lieutenancy}
1191	(directory name).
1192	Unlike
1193	{burp_regime}
1194	(operator-authored config),
1195	{burw_regime}
1196	is system-written by
1197	{busn_conscript}
1198	and consumed by
1199	{busn_discharge},

file.content:
== Regime Configuration

BUK defines two
{axrg_regime_s}
for project and developer configuration.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use
{axf_bash}
format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).

=== Regime Prefixes

[[burc_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burc_prefix}::
Bash Utility Configuration Regime

[[burs_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burs_prefix}::
Bash Utility Station Regime

[[burn_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burn_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Node

[[bure_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{bure_prefix}::
Bash Utility Environment Regime

=== Kit Regime Operations

[[bukro_kindle]]
//axvr_kindle
{bukro_kindle}::
Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
{axrg_assignment_s}.
Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.

[[bukro_validate]]
//axvr_validate
{bukro_validate}::
Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime_s}.
Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.

[[bukro_render]]
//axvr_render
{bukro_render}::
Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime}
state.

=== BURC Regime

[[burc_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{burc_regime}::
Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.

[[burc_station_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_station_file}::
Path to the developer's personal
{burs_regime}
assignment file.

[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
Directory containing
{busdl_launcher}
scripts.

[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
Single-character token separator in
{bust_tabtarget}
inscriptions.

[[burc_tools_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_tools_dir}::
Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.

[[burc_project_root]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_project_root}::
Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.

[[burc_managed_kits]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burc_managed_kits}::
Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.

[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_temp_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK temporary files.

[[burc_output_root_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_output_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK command output files.

[[burc_log_last]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burc_log_last}::
Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.

[[burc_log_ext]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burc_log_ext}::
File extension for log files, without leading dot.

=== BURS Regime

[[burs_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{burs_regime}::
Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.

[[burs_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burs_user}::
Local developer username.
Routes to the correct `.buk/rbmu_users/` subdirectory for
{burp_regime}
profiles.

[[burs_log_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burs_log_dir}::
Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.

=== BURN Regime

[[burn_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burn_regime}::
Per-node configuration regime describing the shape of a remote target.
A
{burn_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burn_viceroyalty}
(directory name) and is shared across station users (git-transported);
it carries no key material and changes rarely.
+
{burn_regime}
files live under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/burn.env`.
+
{burn_regime}
is the sole home of node address-of-record per
{burn_host_singularity};
{burp_regime}
and
{burw_regime}
instances reference it via
{burp_node}
and
{burw_node}.

[[burn_host]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_host}::
IP address or hostname of the remote node.

[[burn_platform]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_platform}::
OS family of the node.
Selects procedure dispatch for OS-specific ceremonies
(garrison shell selection, sshd configuration paths, quoting regime).
Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.
+
Admin-shell variant on Windows nodes (cygwin / wsl / powershell) is
captured in
{burp_command}
per investiture, not at the node level — a single Windows host can
support multiple admin-shell stacks under separate
{burp_investiture}
instances.
Workload-shell variant is captured in
{burw_lieutenancy}
naming.

[[burn_workloads]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated
{burn_workloads}::
Authoritative template of valid
{burw_lieutenancy}
strings on this node.
{busn_conscript}
may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.

=== BURN/BURP/BURW Identifier Vocabulary

The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
BURN/BURP/BURW model.

[[burn_viceroyalty]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burn_viceroyalty}::
The minted identifier of a
{burn_regime}
instance.
{burp_regime}
and
{burw_regime}
instances reference a node by its
{burn_viceroyalty}.
+
The
{burn_viceroyalty}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burn_regime}
assignment file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.

[[burp_investiture]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burp_investiture}::
The minted identifier of a
{burp_regime}
instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
{burn_viceroyalty}.
+
The
{burp_investiture}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burp_regime}
assignment file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.

[[burw_lieutenancy]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burw_lieutenancy}::
The minted identifier of a
{burw_regime}
instance — a workload office operating on a
{burn_viceroyalty}
under the authority of a
{burp_investiture}.
A
{burw_lieutenancy}
names a workload entry (shell command, remote OS user, key file) that
materializes when a workload is conscripted.
+
The
{burw_lieutenancy}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burw_regime}
state file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
The
{burw_lieutenancy}
string is by construction the remote OS username of its workload
account on the node, drawn at conscript time from the
{burn_viceroyalty}'s authoritative `burn_workloads` template.
+
Examples: `redbox-cyg`, `redbox-wsl`, `redbox-pwsh`.

[[burn_host_singularity]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{burn_host_singularity}::
The host of a node lives only in
{burn_regime}.
{burp_regime}
and
{burw_regime}
instances reach the host by reference
(via
{burp_node}
and
{burw_node}
respectively) and never duplicate it.
A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
credentials and workload state never re-state it.

[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
keys on the operator's station.
Regime files carry only the public-key line;
private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
BUK procedures.

=== BURP Regime

[[burp_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burp_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Privileged

[[burp_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burp_regime}::
Per-station-user privileged-credential regime.
A
{burp_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burp_investiture}
(directory name).
+
{burp_regime}
files live under
`.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/burp.env`.
+
{burp_regime}
references its node by
{burp_node}
per
{burn_host_singularity}.

[[burp_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burp_node}::
Reference to a
{burn_viceroyalty}
— the node this investiture is granted over.
Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name.

[[burp_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burp_user}::
Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as
(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
Linux/Mac).

[[burp_ssh_pubkey]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burp_ssh_pubkey}::
Full SSH public key line of the operator-managed admin keypair.
The matching private key lives at `${BURS_KEY_DIR}/<{burp_investiture}>` on
the operator's station per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.

[[burp_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burp_key_file}::
Operator-managed SSH private key filename.
Resolves under the operator's key directory (typically `~/.ssh/`) per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
Empty defaults to the
{burp_investiture}
(one key per investiture).

[[burp_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burp_command}::
Shell command for SSH `command=` routing in the node's
`administrators_authorized_keys`.
Empty for direct-shell connections;
non-empty when the admin shell is selected at the SSH layer
(e.g., `powershell.exe`, `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l`).

=== BURW Regime

[[burw_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burw_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Workload

[[burw_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burw_regime}::
Per-station-user workload-state regime — a record of a workload office
materialized on a node by
{busn_conscript}.
A
{burw_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burw_lieutenancy}
(directory name).
Unlike
{burp_regime}
(operator-authored config),
{burw_regime}
is system-written by
{busn_conscript}
and consumed by
{busn_discharge},

content.content:
1200	{bust_knock},
1201	{bust_remote_run},
1202	and
1203	{bust_interactive_session}.
1204	+
1205	{burw_regime}
1206	references its node by
1207	{burw_node}
1208	per
1209	{burn_host_singularity}.
1210	+
1211	{burw_regime}
1212	files live under
1213	{burs_workload_state_dir}.
1214	
1215	[[burw_node]]
1216	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1217	{burw_node}::
1218	Reference to a
1219	{burn_viceroyalty}
1220	— the node this lieutenancy operates on.
1221	Resolves to the
1222	{burn_regime}
1223	file at the directory bearing this name;
1224	provides the host for SSH operations against the workload account.
1225	
1226	[[burw_command]]
1227	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1228	{burw_command}::
1229	Shell command captured at conscript time, mirroring the `command=`
1230	directive installed in the workload account's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`
1231	on the node.
1232	The conscript-platform variant supplies this template value at
1233	materialization.
1234	
1235	=== BURD Regime
1236	
1237	[[burd_prefix]]
1238	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1239	{burd_prefix}::
1240	Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime
1241	
1242	[[burd_regime]]
1243	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
1244	{burd_regime}::
1245	Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
1246	Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
1247	The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.
1248	
1249	==== Input Variables
1250	
1251	[[burd_group_input]]
1252	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
1253	{burd_group_input}::
1254	Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.
1255	
1256	[[burd_regime_file]]
1257	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1258	{burd_regime_file}::
1259	Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.
1260	
1261	[[burd_no_log]]
1262	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1263	{burd_no_log}::
1264	When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.
1265	
1266	[[burd_interactive]]
1267	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1268	{burd_interactive}::
1269	When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.
1270	
1271	[[burd_coordinator_script]]
1272	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
1273	{burd_coordinator_script}::
1274	Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.
1275	
1276	[[burd_launcher]]
1277	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
1278	{burd_launcher}::
1279	Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.
1280	
1281	[[burd_station_file]]
1282	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
1283	{burd_station_file}::
1284	Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
1285	Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.
1286	
1287	[[burd_term_cols]]
1288	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1289	{burd_term_cols}::
1290	Terminal column width at dispatch time.
1291	Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.
1292	
1293	==== Computed Variables
1294	
1295	[[burd_group_computed]]
1296	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
1297	{burd_group_computed}::
1298	Variables derived during dispatch setup.
1299	
1300	[[burd_now_stamp]]
1301	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1302	{burd_now_stamp}::
1303	Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.
1304	
1305	[[burd_now_epoch]]
1306	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1307	{burd_now_epoch}::
1308	UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.
1309	
1310	[[burd_temp_dir]]
1311	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1312	{burd_temp_dir}::
1313	Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.
1314	
1315	[[burd_output_dir]]
1316	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1317	{burd_output_dir}::
1318	Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.
1319	
1320	[[burd_transcript]]
1321	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1322	{burd_transcript}::
1323	Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.
1324	
1325	[[burd_git_context]]
1326	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1327	{burd_git_context}::
1328	Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.
1329	
1330	[[burd_log_last]]
1331	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1332	{burd_log_last}::
1333	Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.
1334	
1335	[[burd_log_same]]
1336	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1337	{burd_log_same}::
1338	Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.
1339	
1340	[[burd_log_hist]]
1341	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1342	{burd_log_hist}::
1343	Path to the timestamped historical log file.
1344	
1345	==== Parsed Variables
1346	
1347	[[burd_group_parsed]]
1348	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
1349	{burd_group_parsed}::
1350	Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.
1351	
1352	[[burd_command]]
1353	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1354	{burd_command}::
1355	Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).
1356	
1357	[[burd_target]]
1358	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1359	{burd_target}::
1360	Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.
1361	
1362	[[burd_cli_args]]
1363	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated
1364	{burd_cli_args}::
1365	Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.
1366	
1367	[[burd_token_1]]
1368	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1369	{burd_token_1}::
1370	Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).
1371	
1372	[[burd_token_2]]
1373	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1374	{burd_token_2}::
1375	Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).
1376	
1377	[[burd_token_3]]
1378	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1379	{burd_token_3}::
1380	Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).
1381	
1382	[[burd_token_4]]
1383	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1384	{burd_token_4}::
1385	Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.
1386	
1387	[[burd_token_5]]
1388	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1389	{burd_token_5}::
1390	Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.
1391	
1392	=== BURE Regime
1393	
1394	[[bure_regime]]
1395	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
1396	{bure_regime}::
1397	Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.

1422	==== Tweak Mechanism
1423	
1424	The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
1425	BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

1430	pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.

1436	Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.

1447	{bure_tweak_name}.

file.content:
{bust_knock},
{bust_remote_run},
and
{bust_interactive_session}.
+
{burw_regime}
references its node by
{burw_node}
per
{burn_host_singularity}.
+
{burw_regime}
files live under
{burs_workload_state_dir}.

[[burw_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burw_node}::
Reference to a
{burn_viceroyalty}
— the node this lieutenancy operates on.
Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name;
provides the host for SSH operations against the workload account.

[[burw_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burw_command}::
Shell command captured at conscript time, mirroring the `command=`
directive installed in the workload account's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`
on the node.
The conscript-platform variant supplies this template value at
materialization.

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

==== Input Variables

[[burd_group_input]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_input}::
Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.

[[burd_regime_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_regime_file}::
Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.

[[burd_no_log]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_no_log}::
When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.

[[burd_interactive]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_interactive}::
When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.

[[burd_coordinator_script]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_coordinator_script}::
Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.

[[burd_launcher]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_launcher}::
Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.

[[burd_station_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_station_file}::
Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

[[burd_term_cols]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_term_cols}::
Terminal column width at dispatch time.
Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.

==== Computed Variables

[[burd_group_computed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_computed}::
Variables derived during dispatch setup.

[[burd_now_stamp]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_now_stamp}::
Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.

[[burd_now_epoch]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_now_epoch}::
UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.

[[burd_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_temp_dir}::
Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.

[[burd_output_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_output_dir}::
Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.

[[burd_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_transcript}::
Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.

[[burd_git_context]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_git_context}::
Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.

[[burd_log_last]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_last}::
Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.

[[burd_log_same]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_same}::
Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.

[[burd_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_hist}::
Path to the timestamped historical log file.

==== Parsed Variables

[[burd_group_parsed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_parsed}::
Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.

[[burd_command]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_command}::
Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).

[[burd_target]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_target}::
Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.

[[burd_cli_args]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated
{burd_cli_args}::
Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.

[[burd_token_1]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_1}::
Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).

[[burd_token_2]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_2}::
Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).

[[burd_token_3]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_3}::
Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).

[[burd_token_4]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_4}::
Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.

[[burd_token_5]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_5}::
Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.

=== BURE Regime

[[bure_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{bure_regime}::
Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.

[[bure_countdown]]
//axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_countdown}::
Countdown behavior override.
When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
Any other value fails validation.

[[bure_verbose]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{bure_verbose}::
Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.

[[bure_color]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{bure_color}::
Color output policy.
Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.

==== Tweak Mechanism

The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
The caller sets at most one
{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.

[[bure_tweak_name]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_name}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

[[bure_tweak_value]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_value}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
{bure_tweak_name}.
BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.

==== Exchange Labels

[[bure_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname axd_optional
{bure_label}::
Correlation label for cross-project exchange.
When set, propagated verbatim into
{burx_label}
during dispatch.
When unset, BUD writes empty string.
Maximum 120 characters, xname format.
+
BUD writes whatever
{bure_label}
contains without interpreting the semantics.

=== BURX Regime

[[burx_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burx_prefix}::
Bash Utility Exchange Regime

[[burx_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burx_regime}::
Exchange regime for cross-project dispatch state.
Written by bud_dispatch.sh to both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}
as `burx.env` via
{buf_write_fact_single}.
Unlike single-project regimes,
{burx_regime}
is designed to be read across a remote project boundary.
+
Variables appear in two phases:
initial fields are written at dispatch start;
terminal fields are appended at dispatch completion.
Absence of
{burx_exit_status}
indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_pid]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burx_pid}::
Process ID of the dispatched command.

[[burx_began_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_began_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch started.
Format: `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN`.

[[burx_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_tabtarget}::
The tabtarget that was dispatched.
Corresponds to
{burd_target}.

[[burx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_temp_dir}::
Absolute path to the dispatch temporary directory.
Corresponds to
{burd_temp_dir}.

[[burx_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_transcript}::
Absolute path to the transcript file.
Corresponds to
{burd_transcript}.

[[burx_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_log_hist}::
Absolute path to the historical log file.
Corresponds to
{burd_log_hist}.

[[burx_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_label}::
Correlation label from
{bure_label}.
Always present — empty string when
{bure_label}
is not set.
Format and interpretation are opaque to BUD.

[[burx_exit_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer axd_optional
{burx_exit_status}::
Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_ended_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

Privilege is encoded by regime, not by a field on
{burn_regime}.
Each
{burp_regime}
instance grants a single station user privileged authority over a
{burn_viceroyalty},
and each
{burw_regime}
instance records an ephemeral workload user materialized on that
viceroyalty by
{busn_conscript}.
Tabtargets dispatch by colophon family
(`buw-np*` for privileged-tier verbs,
`buw-nw*` for workload-tier verbs)
and consume the regime appropriate to the verb.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged operations authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or
equivalent privileges via a
{burp_regime}
instance.
Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user provisioning,
node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is

content.content:
1600	enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.
1601	
1602	==== Workload Tier
1603	
1604	Workload operations authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1605	privileged authority and tracked by a
1606	{burw_regime}
1607	instance.
1608	Used for normal remote work: running commands, file operations,
1609	interactive shells.
1610	
1611	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1612	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1613	{busn_conscript}
1614	when needed,
1615	{busn_discharge}
1616	when done.

1621	operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.
