file.content:
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload operations authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority and tracked by a
{burw_regime}
instance.
Used for normal remote work: running commands, file operations,
interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile (investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile (investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)

[[bust_garrison_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_linux}::
`buw-npgl` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.

[[bust_garrison_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_mac}::
`buw-npgm` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-npgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-npgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.

[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_powershell}::
`buw-npgp` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.

[[bust_garrison_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_localhost}::
`buw-npgx` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for localhost nodes.

==== Workload (privileged authority required)

[[bust_conscript_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_linux}::
`buw-nwcl` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Linux shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_mac}::
`buw-nwcm` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a macOS shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
`buw-nwcc` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_wsl}::
`buw-nwcw` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_powershell}::
`buw-nwcp` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_localhost}::
`buw-nwcx` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_discharge]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_discharge}::
`buw-nwd` —
{busn_discharge} a workload user.
Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
authority and removes the local profile.

[[bust_inventory]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_inventory}::
`buw-nwi` —
{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
privileged BURN.

==== Workload (operational)

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-nwk` —
Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.

[[bust_remote_run]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_remote_run}::
`buw-nwr` —
Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
{burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-nws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<burs_user>/<investiture>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its privileged-credential subtree exclusively, and operations
write only under the current station user's tree.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::
List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_remote_run}.
Path passed as the second positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

include::BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc[]

include::BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc[]

include::BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc[]

include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSTGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSTGP-GarrisonPowershell.adoc[]

include::BUSTCC-ConscriptCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSTCW-ConscriptWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSTCP-ConscriptPowershell.adoc[]

include::BUSTWD-Discharge.adoc[]

include::BUSTWI-Inventory.adoc[]

include::BUSTOK-Knock.adoc[]

include::BUSTOE-RemoteRun.adoc[]

include::BUSTOS-InteractiveSession.adoc[]

include::BUSTHT-HandbookNodeTop.adoc[]

content.text:
Inventory complete. Now I have the full picture. Key findings:

**Mapping section structure (lines 4-255):**
- Lines 38-62: Concrete BURN/BURP tabtarget refs (most need replacement)
- Lines 117-120: BURN regime (drop `burn_workloads`)
- Lines 122-125: Identifier vocabulary (drop `burw_lieutenancy`)
- Lines 131-137: BURP regime quoins (rewrite to four-field shape)
- Lines 139-142: BURW regime (delete entirely)
- Lines 144-148: BUSN verbs (drop `busn_conscript`, `busn_discharge`, `busn_inventory`; keep/replace `busn_garrison`)

**Body sections requiring change:**
- BURN regime body: `burn_platform` references `burp_command` and `burw_lieutenancy` (lines 974-1001)
- BURP regime body: lines 1140-1173 need full rewrite
- BURW regime body: lines 1174-1234 → delete
- §Remote Node Access: lines 1564-1842 → comprehensive rewrite
- Include directives: lines 1844-1884

**Subdoc disposition decision needed:** docket retires BUSTGC/GW/GP, BUSTCC/CW/CP, BUSTWD/WI, BUSNW/NL/NM (11). But BUSTOK/OE/OS/HT have colophons changing (`buw-nw*` → `buw-j*`); cleanest is to retire those too and mint 7 new BUSJ* with jurisdiction-themed naming. Will flag this when it lands.

Updating tasks and proceeding with mapping section edits.

content.content:
920	[[burs_log_dir]]
921	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
922	{burs_log_dir}::
923	Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.
924	
925	=== BURN Regime
926	
927	[[burn_regime]]
928	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
929	{burn_regime}::
930	Per-node configuration regime describing the shape of a remote target.
931	A
932	{burn_regime}
933	instance is identified by its
934	{burn_viceroyalty}
935	(directory name) and is shared across station users (git-transported);
936	it carries no key material and changes rarely.
937	+
938	{burn_regime}
939	files live under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/burn.env`.
940	+
941	{burn_regime}
942	is the sole home of node address-of-record per
943	{burn_host_singularity};
944	{burp_regime}
945	and
946	{burw_regime}
947	instances reference it via
948	{burp_node}
949	and
950	{burw_node}.
951	
952	[[burn_host]]
953	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
954	{burn_host}::
955	IP address or hostname of the remote node.
956	
957	[[burn_platform]]
958	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
959	{burn_platform}::
960	OS family of the node.
961	Selects procedure dispatch for OS-specific ceremonies
962	(garrison shell selection, sshd configuration paths, quoting regime).
963	Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.
964	+
965	Admin-shell variant on Windows nodes (cygwin / wsl / powershell) is
966	captured in
967	{burp_command}
968	per investiture, not at the node level — a single Windows host can
969	support multiple admin-shell stacks under separate
970	{burp_investiture}
971	instances.
972	Workload-shell variant is captured in
973	{burw_lieutenancy}
974	naming.
975	
976	[[burn_workloads]]
977	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated
978	{burn_workloads}::
979	Authoritative template of valid
980	{burw_lieutenancy}
981	strings on this node.
982	{busn_conscript}
983	may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
984	the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.
985	
986	=== BURN/BURP/BURW Identifier Vocabulary
987	
988	The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
989	BURN/BURP/BURW model.
990	
991	[[burn_viceroyalty]]
992	//axl_voices axo_entity
993	{burn_viceroyalty}::
994	The minted identifier of a
995	{burn_regime}
996	instance.
997	{burp_regime}
998	and
999	{burw_regime}
1000	instances reference a node by its
1001	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1002	+
1003	The
1004	{burn_viceroyalty}
1005	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1006	{burn_regime}
1007	assignment file;
1008	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1009	+
1010	Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.
1011	
1012	[[burp_investiture]]
1013	//axl_voices axo_entity
1014	{burp_investiture}::
1015	The minted identifier of a
1016	{burp_regime}
1017	instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
1018	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1019	+
1020	The
1021	{burp_investiture}
1022	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1023	{burp_regime}
1024	assignment file;
1025	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1026	+
1027	Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.
1028	
1029	[[burw_lieutenancy]]
1030	//axl_voices axo_entity
1031	{burw_lieutenancy}::
1032	The minted identifier of a
1033	{burw_regime}
1034	instance — a workload office operating on a
1035	{burn_viceroyalty}
1036	under the authority of a
1037	{burp_investiture}.
1038	A
1039	{burw_lieutenancy}
1040	names a workload entry (shell command, remote OS user, key file) that
1041	materializes when a workload is conscripted.
1042	+
1043	The
1044	{burw_lieutenancy}
1045	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1046	{burw_regime}
1047	state file;
1048	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1049	+
1050	The
1051	{burw_lieutenancy}
1052	string is by construction the remote OS username of its workload
1053	account on the node, drawn at conscript time from the
1054	{burn_viceroyalty}'s authoritative `burn_workloads` template.
1055	+
1056	Examples: `redbox-cyg`, `redbox-wsl`, `redbox-pwsh`.
1057	
1058	[[burn_host_singularity]]
1059	//axl_voices axk_premise
1060	{burn_host_singularity}::
1061	The host of a node lives only in
1062	{burn_regime}.
1063	{burp_regime}
1064	and
1065	{burw_regime}
1066	instances reach the host by reference
1067	(via
1068	{burp_node}
1069	and
1070	{burw_node}
1071	respectively) and never duplicate it.
1072	A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
1073	credentials and workload state never re-state it.
1074	
1075	[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
1076	//axl_voices axk_premise
1077	{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
1078	The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
1079	The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
1080	distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
1081	keys on the operator's station.
1082	Regime files carry only the public-key line;
1083	private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
1084	BUK procedures.

[[burp_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burp_key_file}::
Operator-managed SSH private key filename.
Resolves under the operator's key directory (typically `~/.ssh/`) per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
Empty defaults to the
{burp_investiture}
(one key per investiture).

[[burp_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burp_command}::
Shell command for SSH `command=` routing in the node's
`administrators_authorized_keys`.
Empty for direct-shell connections;
non-empty when the admin shell is selected at the SSH layer
(e.g., `powershell.exe`, `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l`).

=== BURW Regime

[[burw_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burw_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Workload

[[burw_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burw_regime}::
Per-station-user workload-state regime — a record of a workload office
materialized on a node by
{busn_conscript}.
A
{burw_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burw_lieutenancy}
(directory name).
Unlike
{burp_regime}
(operator-authored config),
{burw_regime}
is system-written by
{busn_conscript}
and consumed by
{busn_discharge},
{bust_knock},
{bust_remote_run},
and
{bust_interactive_session}.
+
{burw_regime}
references its node by
{burw_node}
per
{burn_host_singularity}.
+
{burw_regime}
files live under
{burs_workload_state_dir}.

[[burw_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burw_node}::
Reference to a
{burn_viceroyalty}
— the node this lieutenancy operates on.
Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name;
provides the host for SSH operations against the workload account.

[[burw_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burw_command}::
Shell command captured at conscript time, mirroring the `command=`
directive installed in the workload account's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`
on the node.
The conscript-platform variant supplies this template value at
materialization.

=== BURD Regime

//axl_voices axrg_prefix

Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton

//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_platform}::
OS family of the node.
Selects procedure dispatch for OS-specific ceremonies
(garrison shell selection, sshd configuration paths, quoting regime).
Values: `linux`, `mac`, `windows`.
+
Admin-shell variant on Windows nodes (cygwin / wsl / powershell) is
captured in
{burp_command}
per investiture, not at the node level — a single Windows host can
support multiple admin-shell stacks under separate
{burp_investiture}
instances.
Workload-shell variant is captured in
{burw_lieutenancy}
naming.

//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated

Authoritative template of valid
{burw_lieutenancy}
strings on this node.
{busn_conscript}
may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.

=== BURN/BURP/BURW Identifier Vocabulary

The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
BURN/BURP/BURW model.

input.new_string:
[[burp_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burp_regime}::
Per-station-user credential regime carrying both privileged (admin)
and workload key references for a single
{burn_viceroyalty}.
A
{burp_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burp_investiture}
(directory name).
+
{burp_regime}
files live under
`.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/burp.env`.
+
{burp_regime}
references its node by
{burp_node}
per
{burn_host_singularity}.
+
Public keys are derived from private keys at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
neither the privileged nor the workload pubkey is stored in
{burp_regime}.
Encrypted (passphrase-protected) private keys are not supported because
`ssh-keygen -y` would prompt interactively and hang the ceremony.

[[burp_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burp_node}::
Reference to a
{burn_viceroyalty}
— the node this investiture is granted over.
Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name.

[[burp_privileged_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burp_privileged_user}::
Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as for the
privileged garrison ceremony
(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
Linux/Mac).
Garrison runs against this account to provision the workload account.
+
Some platforms permit privileged usernames containing spaces
(e.g., `b hyslop`); the implementation must quote correctly at every
expansion.

[[burp_privileged_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_privileged_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key authorized as
{burp_privileged_user}
on the node per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
The matching public key is derived at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
no separate pubkey path is stored.
+
Garrison authenticates using this key only — there is no password fallback.
First-time admin trust is established out-of-band via the bootstrap
handbook before garrison runs.

[[burp_workload_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_workload_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key the workload account uses
to authenticate inbound (from the operator's station) and outbound
(to GitHub or other services).
Garrison copies this private key onto the remote workload account at a
shell-letter-specific path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction};
the matching public key is derived via `ssh-keygen -y` for
authorized_keys placement on both sides of the trip.
+
The workload pubkey must be pre-registered with any outbound service
(e.g., GitHub deploy keys) out-of-band; garrison cannot validate that
registration.

=== BURD Regime

Some platforms permit privileged usernames containing spaces
(e.g., `b hyslop`); the implementation must quote correctly at every
expansion.

[[burp_privileged_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_privileged_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key authorized as
{burp_privileged_user}
on the node per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
The matching public key is derived at use-time via `ssh-keygen -y`;
no separate pubkey path is stored.
+
Garrison authenticates using this key only — there is no password fallback.
First-time admin trust is established out-of-band via the bootstrap
handbook before garrison runs.

[[burp_workload_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_workload_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key the workload account uses
to authenticate inbound (from the operator's station) and outbound
(to GitHub or other services).
Garrison copies this private key onto the remote workload account at a
shell-letter-specific path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction};
the matching public key is derived via `ssh-keygen -y` for
authorized_keys placement on both sides of the trip.
+
The workload pubkey must be pre-registered with any outbound service
(e.g., GitHub deploy keys) out-of-band; garrison cannot validate that
registration.

=== BURD Regime

1456	== Remote Node Access
1457	
1458	Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
1459	trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
1460	workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.
1461	
1462	=== Privilege Tiers
1463	
1464	Privilege is encoded by regime, not by a field on
1465	{burn_regime}.
1466	Each
1467	{burp_regime}
1468	instance grants a single station user privileged authority over a
1469	{burn_viceroyalty},
1470	and each
1471	{burw_regime}
1472	instance records an ephemeral workload user materialized on that
1473	viceroyalty by
1474	{busn_conscript}.
1475	Tabtargets dispatch by colophon family
1476	(`buw-np*` for privileged-tier verbs,
1477	`buw-nw*` for workload-tier verbs)
1478	and consume the regime appropriate to the verb.
1479	
1480	==== Privileged Tier
1481	
1482	Privileged operations authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or
1483	equivalent privileges via a
1484	{burp_regime}
1485	instance.
1486	Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user provisioning,
1487	node-establishment ceremonies.
1488	
1489	Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
1490	format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
1491	station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
1492	enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.
1493	
1494	==== Workload Tier
1495	
1496	Workload operations authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1497	privileged authority and tracked by a
1498	{burw_regime}
1499	instance.
1500	Used for normal remote work: running commands, file operations,
1501	interactive shells.
1502	
1503	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1504	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1505	{busn_conscript}
1506	when needed,
1507	{busn_discharge}
1508	when done.
1509	
1510	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1511	
1512	Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
1513	operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.
1514	
1515	==== Lifecycle
1516	
1517	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1518	//axvo_tabtarget
1519	{bust_node_regime_list}::
1520	`buw-rnl` —
1521	List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.
1522	
1523	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1524	//axvo_tabtarget
1525	{bust_node_regime_render}::
1526	`buw-rnr` —
1527	Render a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1528	
1529	[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
1530	//axvo_tabtarget
1531	{bust_node_regime_validate}::
1532	`buw-rnv` —
1533	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).
1534	
1535	[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
1536	//axvo_tabtarget
1537	{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
1538	`buw-rpl` —
1539	List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.
1540	
1541	[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
1542	//axvo_tabtarget
1543	{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
1544	`buw-rpr` —
1545	Render a single {burp_regime} profile (investiture passed as positional argument).
1546	
1547	[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
1548	//axvo_tabtarget
1549	{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
1550	`buw-rpv` —
1551	Validate a single {burp_regime} profile (investiture passed as positional argument).
1552	
1553	==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)
1554	
1555	[[bust_garrison_linux]]
1556	//axvo_tabtarget
1557	{bust_garrison_linux}::
1558	`buw-npgl` —
1559	{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.
1560	
1561	[[bust_garrison_mac]]
1562	//axvo_tabtarget
1563	{bust_garrison_mac}::
1564	`buw-npgm` —
1565	{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.
1566	
1567	[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
1568	//axvo_tabtarget
1569	{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
1570	`buw-npgc` —
1571	{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.
1572	
1573	[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
1574	//axvo_tabtarget
1575	{bust_garrison_wsl}::
1576	`buw-npgw` —
1577	{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.
1578	
1579	[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
1580	//axvo_tabtarget
1581	{bust_garrison_powershell}::
1582	`buw-npgp` —
1583	{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.
1584	
1585	[[bust_garrison_localhost]]
1586	//axvo_tabtarget
1587	{bust_garrison_localhost}::
1588	`buw-npgx` —
1589	{busn_garrison} ceremony for localhost nodes.
1590	
1591	==== Workload (privileged authority required)
1592	
1593	[[bust_conscript_linux]]
1594	//axvo_tabtarget
1595	{bust_conscript_linux}::
1596	`buw-nwcl` —
1597	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Linux shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1598	
1599	[[bust_conscript_mac]]
1600	//axvo_tabtarget
1601	{bust_conscript_mac}::
1602	`buw-nwcm` —
1603	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a macOS shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1604	
1605	[[bust_conscript_cygwin]]
1606	//axvo_tabtarget
1607	{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
1608	`buw-nwcc` —
1609	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1610	
1611	[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
1612	//axvo_tabtarget
1613	{bust_conscript_wsl}::
1614	`buw-nwcw` —
1615	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1616	
1617	[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
1618	//axvo_tabtarget
1619	{bust_conscript_powershell}::
1620	`buw-nwcp` —
1621	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1622	
1623	[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
1624	//axvo_tabtarget
1625	{bust_conscript_localhost}::
1626	`buw-nwcx` —
1627	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1628	
1629	[[bust_discharge]]
1630	//axvo_tabtarget
1631	{bust_discharge}::
1632	`buw-nwd` —
1633	{busn_discharge} a workload user.
1634	Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
1635	authority and removes the local profile.
1636	
1637	[[bust_inventory]]
1638	//axvo_tabtarget
1639	{bust_inventory}::
1640	`buw-nwi` —
1641	{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
1642	privileged BURN.
1643	
1644	==== Workload (operational)
1645	
1646	[[bust_knock]]
1647	//axvo_tabtarget
1648	{bust_knock}::
1649	`buw-nwk` —
1650	Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.
1651	
1652	[[bust_remote_run]]
1653	//axvo_tabtarget
1654	{bust_remote_run}::
1655	`buw-nwr` —
1656	Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
1657	{burd_output_dir}.
1658	
1659	[[bust_interactive_session]]
1660	//axvo_tabtarget
1661	{bust_interactive_session}::
1662	`buw-nws` —
1663	Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.
1664	
1665	==== Handbook
1666	
1667	[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
1668	//axvo_tabtarget
1669	{bust_handbook_node_top}::
1670	`buw-hn0` —
1671	Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.
1672	
1673	=== Privileges
1674	
1675	A
1676	{burn_regime}
1677	instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/` and is shared
1678	across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
1679	no key material.
1680	A
1681	{burp_regime}
1682	instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<burs_user>/<investiture>/`;
1683	the
1684	{burs_user}
1685	owns its privileged-credential subtree exclusively, and operations
1686	write only under the current station user's tree.
1687	
1688	Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
1689	ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
1690	the Administrators group).
1691	
1692	=== Verb Definitions
1693	
1694	[[busn_garrison]]
1695	//axl_voices axt_command
1696	{busn_garrison}::
1697	Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
1698	{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
1699	admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
1700	node setup; idempotent on re-run.
1701	
1702	[[busn_conscript]]
1703	//axl_voices axt_command
1704	{busn_conscript}::
1705	Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
1706	{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
1707	the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
1708	where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
1709	privileged BURN's shell choice.
1710	
1711	[[busn_discharge]]
1712	//axl_voices axt_command
1713	{busn_discharge}::
1714	Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
1715	{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
1716	authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
1717	user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.
1718	
1719	[[busn_inventory]]
1720	//axl_voices axt_command
1721	{busn_inventory}::
1722	List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
1723	privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
1724	just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.
1725	
1726	=== Input Artifacts
1727	
1728	[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
1729	//axl_voices axo_entity
1730	{busi_scribed_command_file}::
1731	A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
1732	on the remote node by {bust_remote_run}.
1733	Path passed as the second positional argument; contents are streamed
1734	to the remote shell at dispatch time.
1735	
1736	include::BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc[]
1737	
1738	include::BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc[]
1739	
1740	include::BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc[]
1741	
1742	include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]
1743	
1744	include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]
1745	
1746	include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]
1747	
1748	include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]
1749	
1750	include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]
1751	
1752	include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]
1753	
1754	include::BUSTGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

Privilege is encoded by regime, not by a field on
{burn_regime}.
Each
{burp_regime}
instance grants a single station user privileged authority over a
{burn_viceroyalty},
and each
{burw_regime}
instance records an ephemeral workload user materialized on that
viceroyalty by
{busn_conscript}.
Tabtargets dispatch by colophon family
(`buw-np*` for privileged-tier verbs,
`buw-nw*` for workload-tier verbs)
and consume the regime appropriate to the verb.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged operations authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or
equivalent privileges via a
{burp_regime}
instance.
Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user provisioning,
node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload operations authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority and tracked by a
{burw_regime}
instance.
Used for normal remote work: running commands, file operations,
interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

Privilege is encoded by regime, not by a field on
{burn_regime}.
Each
{burp_regime}
instance grants a single station user privileged authority over a
{burn_viceroyalty},
and each
{burw_regime}
instance records an ephemeral workload user materialized on that
viceroyalty by
{busn_conscript}.
Tabtargets dispatch by colophon family
(`buw-np*` for privileged-tier verbs,
`buw-nw*` for workload-tier verbs)
and consume the regime appropriate to the verb.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged operations authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or
equivalent privileges via a
{burp_regime}
instance.
Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user provisioning,
node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload operations authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority and tracked by a
{burw_regime}
instance.
Used for normal remote work: running commands, file operations,
interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

input.new_string:
== Remote Node Access

BUK reaches remote nodes under the
{bus_jurisdiction}
umbrella: a single station user provisions one workload account per
node and dispatches commands to it. Privileged trust is the operator's
prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
is an out-of-band handbook procedure.

[[bus_jurisdiction]]
{bus_jurisdiction}::
The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically:
operator-managed admin trust as input,
{busn_garrison}
to provision and refresh a workload account, and the workload-side
tabtargets ({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
to dispatch work.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
{bujb_jurisdiction}::
The BCG-compliant module
(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
that hardcodes the
{bus_jurisdiction}
contracts not expressed in regime data:
the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
`c` = Cygwin;
`w` = WSL),
the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
and the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`).
+
SSH transport mechanics live in code;
{burp_regime}
carries no shell selection.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
under the current station user's tree.

Garrison authenticates as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
(key auth only — no password fallback).
Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
{burc_workload_user}
(project-wide convention, single source of truth).

=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Conscript / discharge / withdraw / vanquish / inventory verbs are
absent from this model — garrison subsumes them by recreating from a
clean slate on every run.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Thirteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, garrison ceremony, and
workload dispatch.
PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.

==== Regime Configuration

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Garrison (per shell-letter)

[[bust_garrison_bash]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_bash}::
`buw-jpgb` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
(Linux and macOS nodes).

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-jpgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via Cygwin sshd).

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-jpgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via WSL distribution `rbtww-main`).

==== Workload Dispatch

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-jwk` —
Probe workload reachability via key-only SSH;
returns 0 if the workload account answers a no-op command.

[[bust_command_file]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_command_file}::
`buw-jwc` —
Run the contents of a local command file as the workload account;
shell is determined by whichever shell-letter garrisoned the node.
Captures stdout, stderr, and remote exit status to {burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-jws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
`buw-hj0` —
Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures
(garrison preparation, bootstrap pointers, dispatch usage).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only.
. Idempotently place admin pubkey
(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
. Destroy any existing workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
directory.
. Create fresh workload account named
{burc_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
per shell-letter,
so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_command_file}.
Path passed as a positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSJGB-GarrisonBash.adoc[]

include::BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSJWK-Knock.adoc[]

include::BUSJWC-CommandFile.adoc[]

include::BUSJWS-InteractiveSession.adoc[]

include::BUSJH0-HandbookJurisdictionTop.adoc[]

{burp_regime}
instance.
Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user provisioning,
node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload operations authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority and tracked by a
{burw_regime}
instance.
Used for normal remote work: running commands, file operations,
interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile (investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile (investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)

[[bust_garrison_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_linux}::
`buw-npgl` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.

[[bust_garrison_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_mac}::
`buw-npgm` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-npgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-npgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.

[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_powershell}::
`buw-npgp` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.

[[bust_garrison_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_localhost}::
`buw-npgx` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for localhost nodes.

==== Workload (privileged authority required)

[[bust_conscript_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_linux}::
`buw-nwcl` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Linux shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_mac}::
`buw-nwcm` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a macOS shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
`buw-nwcc` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_wsl}::
`buw-nwcw` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_powershell}::
`buw-nwcp` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_localhost}::
`buw-nwcx` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_discharge]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_discharge}::
`buw-nwd` —
{busn_discharge} a workload user.
Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
authority and removes the local profile.

[[bust_inventory]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_inventory}::
`buw-nwi` —
{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
privileged BURN.

==== Workload (operational)

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-nwk` —
Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.

[[bust_remote_run]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_remote_run}::
`buw-nwr` —
Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
{burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-nws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<burs_user>/<investiture>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its privileged-credential subtree exclusively, and operations
write only under the current station user's tree.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::
List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_remote_run}.
Path passed as the second positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

toolUseResult.newString:
== Remote Node Access

BUK reaches remote nodes under the
{bus_jurisdiction}
umbrella: a single station user provisions one workload account per
node and dispatches commands to it. Privileged trust is the operator's
prerequisite, not BUK's deliverable — first-time admin SSH bootstrap
is an out-of-band handbook procedure.

[[bus_jurisdiction]]
{bus_jurisdiction}::
The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically:
operator-managed admin trust as input,
{busn_garrison}
to provision and refresh a workload account, and the workload-side
tabtargets ({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
to dispatch work.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
{bujb_jurisdiction}::
The BCG-compliant module
(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
that hardcodes the
{bus_jurisdiction}
contracts not expressed in regime data:
the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
`c` = Cygwin;
`w` = WSL),
the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
and the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`).
+
SSH transport mechanics live in code;
{burp_regime}
carries no shell selection.

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_viceroyalty}>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
under the current station user's tree.

Garrison authenticates as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
(key auth only — no password fallback).
Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
{burc_workload_user}
(project-wide convention, single source of truth).

=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Conscript / discharge / withdraw / vanquish / inventory verbs are
absent from this model — garrison subsumes them by recreating from a
clean slate on every run.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Thirteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, garrison ceremony, and
workload dispatch.
PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.

==== Regime Configuration

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
(viceroyalty passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
(investiture passed as positional argument).

==== Garrison (per shell-letter)

[[bust_garrison_bash]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_bash}::
`buw-jpgb` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with native bash as the workload shell
(Linux and macOS nodes).

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-jpgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via Cygwin sshd).

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-jpgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony with WSL bash as the workload shell
(Windows nodes via WSL distribution `rbtww-main`).

==== Workload Dispatch

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-jwk` —
Probe workload reachability via key-only SSH;
returns 0 if the workload account answers a no-op command.

[[bust_command_file]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_command_file}::
`buw-jwc` —
Run the contents of a local command file as the workload account;
shell is determined by whichever shell-letter garrisoned the node.
Captures stdout, stderr, and remote exit status to {burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-jws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top}::
`buw-hj0` —
Top-level handbook landing for jurisdiction procedures
(garrison preparation, bootstrap pointers, dispatch usage).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish or refresh workload trust on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpgb`, `buw-jpgc`, `buw-jpgw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only.
. Idempotently place admin pubkey
(derived from privkey via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in admin's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=` directive.
. Destroy any existing workload account named
{burc_workload_user}
via `userdel -r` or platform equivalent — removes account and home
directory.
. Create fresh workload account named
{burc_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
per shell-letter,
so the workload can authenticate outbound (e.g., to GitHub).
. Validate round-trip: SSH as workload, exec a no-op, verify exit 0.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_command_file}.
Path passed as a positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTPL-PrivilegeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTPR-PrivilegeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSJGB-GarrisonBash.adoc[]

include::BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSJWK-Knock.adoc[]

include::BUSJWC-CommandFile.adoc[]

include::BUSJWS-InteractiveSession.adoc[]

include::BUSJH0-HandbookJurisdictionTop.adoc[]

file.content:
=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every garrison invocation wipes prior workload state on the node
regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Conscript / discharge / withdraw / vanquish / inventory verbs are
absent from this model — garrison subsumes them by recreating from a
clean slate on every run.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

python3 -c '
import re
text = open("Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc").read()

new_quoins = [
    "bus_jurisdiction","bujb_jurisdiction","burc_workload_user",
    "burp_privileged_user","burp_privileged_key_file","burp_workload_key_file",
    "bust_garrison_bash","bust_garrison_cygwin","bust_garrison_wsl",
    "bust_knock","bust_command_file","bust_interactive_session",
    "bust_handbook_jurisdiction_top",
]

for q in new_quoins:
    in_mapping = re.search(rf"^:{q}:\s+<<{q},", text, re.MULTILINE) is not None
    has_anchor = f"[[{q}]]" in text
    print(f"{q:42s} mapping={in_mapping}  anchor={has_anchor}")
'

    "bust_remote_run","bust_handbook_node_top",
]
print("Surviving references to dropped quoins:")
any_found = False
for q in dropped:

        for ln, line in matches:

if not any_found: print("  (none — clean)")

content.content:
91a75146 jjb:1011-e7d2f2ed:₢BBAAg:n: Final assembly of the gauntlet test suite — rbw-tP release-qualification entry point. Composes the 11-fixture gauntlet ladder (enrollment-validation → pristine-lifecycle → canonical-establish → onboarding-sequence → regime-validation → regime-smoke → four-mode → tadmor → moriah → srjcl → pluml) as a TestSuite-shaped composition over existing infrastructure: ZRBTE_SUITE_GAUNTLET bash array in rbte_engine.sh + gauntlet) resolver branch + tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh launcher. The bash for-loop in rbte_suite() under set -e provides the suite-level fail-fast contract for free; no orchestrator-script needed. rbw-tP wired via rbz_zipper RBZ_QUALIFY_PRISTINE enrollment + rbq_qualify_pristine handler, which delegates contract enforcement to pristine-lifecycle case 1 (the entry-contract gate landed in BBAAB checks tree-clean + RBRR-blank + RBRA-absent + nameplate-hallmarks-empty + vessel-depot-fields-empty in one aggregated diagnostic). Docket prescribed dual enforcement at the rbw-tP layer plus pristine-lifecycle case 1, but BBAAB's final landed implementation explicitly omitted a commit-signature primitive ("no signature trailer") and the 5 violation classes are already enforced exhaustively as the gauntlet's first content fixture; duplicating them in bash would re-implement Rust predicates for cosmetic earlier-failure with maintenance-drift hazard. Operator running rbw-tP without marshal-zero sees enrollment-validation pass quickly (state-indifferent preflight) then pristine-lifecycle case 1 surface the violation diagnostic. Moriah included as a tadmor twin per operator direction: registered RBTDRM_FIXTURE_MORIAH + RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH sharing RBTDRC_SECTIONS_SECURITY (renamed from RBTDRC_SECTIONS_TADMOR) — runtime semantics identical to tadmor; provenance differs (cloud-built airgap vs local kludge). Section names stripped of "tadmor-" prefix to make the array genuinely shareable: 8 sections renamed (basic-infra, ifrit-attacks, observation, correlated, sortie-attacks, unilateral-novel, coordinated-attacks, coordinated-integrity). Theurge-ifrit-context doc table updated with new prefix-free names plus prose noting tadmor+moriah share the array. Build clean under #![deny(warnings)]; fast suite 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing failure unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs/BBAAv baseline; rbw-tf qualify-fast green; tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.moriah.sh now passes manifest verification (responds with vessel-exists precondition surfacing per BBAAs as expected). Tabtarget context regenerated via rbw-MG. Out of scope per docket: BBAAX first-run end-to-end validation, BBAAH pristine-passed sentinel, BBAAI documentation updates beyond what the workbench tabtarget needs. Pre-existing bug not addressed (out of scope, awaiting decision): rbq_qualify_release at rbq_Qualify.sh:183 references tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.complete.sh which doesn't exist — should be tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.complete.sh; would currently fail rbw-tr execution, but rbw-tr presumably hasn't been invoked since the rename.
60a89051 jjb:1011-e4c34615:₢A-AAd:n: add axhopt_typed_parameter markers to 13 BUSTxx subdocs (12 subdocs * burn_alias, BUSTOE * burn_alias + busi_scribed_command_file); mint busi_scribed_command_file quoin in BUS0 with mapping line and definition site under new === Input Artifacts subsection
e4c34615 jjb:1011-31289deb:₢A-AAd:n: draft BUSTxx detail-site subdocs for 15 Windows-relevant BURN tabtargets; wire includes into BUS0; preconditions/steps/guarantees/completion per AXLA axho* hierarchy; 21 voicings unchanged; buw-st passes
---
---

content.text:
All eight done-when criteria are met. Summary of changes:

**BUS0 mapping section** — dropped 16 quoins (BURW family, old BURP fields `burp_user`/`ssh_pubkey`/`key_file`/`command`, old tabtargets, conscript/discharge/inventory verbs, `burn_workloads`, `burs_workload_state_dir`); minted 13 new quoins (`bus_jurisdiction`, `bujb_jurisdiction`, `burc_workload_user`, `burp_privileged_user`/`privileged_key_file`/`workload_key_file`, 7 new tabtarget refs).

**BUS0 body** — rewrote `burn_platform` (no more burp_command/lieutenancy refs); deleted entire BURW Regime section; restructured BURP body to four-field shape with `ssh-keygen -y` derivation note; rewrote `burn_host_singularity` premise; new `=== Garrison-Destructive Model` subsection; replaced §Remote Node Access (Privileges, Tabtarget Catalog with 13 entries, Verb Definitions reduced to garrison-only with full ceremony narrative).

**Subdocs** — retired 15 (11 explicit per docket + 4 implicit BUSTOK/OE/OS/HT whose colophons changed); minted 7 new BUSJ* (Garrison Bash/Cygwin/Wsl, Knock, CommandFile, InteractiveSession, HandbookJurisdictionTop).

**Cross-references** — fixed BUSTPV (was using old BURP fields) and BUSTLV (was requiring `burn_workloads`). These were surviving subdocs that the grep sweep caught.

**Verification clean:**
- All 13 new quoins have full mapping + anchor + body coverage
- Zero surviving references to dropped concepts (lieutenancy, conscript, discharge, withdraw, vanquish, inventory, multi-resident, per-platform garrison)
- All `<<anchor,Display>>` refs resolve
- All 14 include directives resolve to existing files

**One judgment call to flag:** docket retire-list named 11 subdocs explicitly. I added BUSTOK/OE/OS/HT (4 more) because their colophons changed (`buw-nw{k,r,s}` → `buw-j{wk,wc,ws}`; `buw-hn0` → `buw-hj0`) and they referenced dropped quoins, so leaving them would have produced dangling refs. Net subdoc count: −15 / +7. New naming uses `BUSJ` prefix (jurisdiction) consistently for the 7 new files.

Diff stat: BUS0 −430 / +261 lines (679 → ~510). Total file delta: −15 retired, +7 new, 3 modified. Ready for your review — do you want to wrap ₢A-AAl?
